content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_015_page_001.jpg

1. And I make known to you, brothers, the good news which I proclaimed to you, which also you received, in which also you stand,

2. through which also you are being saved, if you hold fast the word which I proclaimed to you — unless you believed in vain.

3. For I delivered to you among [the] first [things] what I also received: that Christos died for the sins of us according to the Scriptures,

4. and that he was buried, and that he has been raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,

5. and that he appeared to Kephas, then to the twelve.

6. Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers at once, of whom the majority remain until now, but some have fallen asleep.

7. Then he appeared to Iakobos, then to all the apostles.

8. And last of all, as to the [one] untimely born, he appeared also to me.

9. For I am the least of the apostles, who am not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.

10. But by [the] grace of God I am what I am, and the grace of him toward me was not empty, but I labored more abundantly than all of them — yet not I but the grace of God with me.

11. Whether then I or they, thus we proclaim and thus you believed.

12. And if Christos is proclaimed that he has been raised from [the] dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of [the] dead?

13. And if there is no resurrection of [the] dead, neither has Christos been raised.

14. And if Christos has not been raised, then empty [is] the proclamation of us, and empty also [is] the faith of you.

15. And we are found also [to be] false witnesses of God, because we testified concerning God that he raised the Christos, whom he did not raise — if indeed [the] dead are not raised.

16. For if [the] dead are not raised, neither has Christos been raised.

17. And if Christos has not been raised, futile [is] the faith of you; you are still in the sins of you.

18. Then also the [ones] having fallen asleep in Christos perished.

19. If in this life only we have hoped in Christos, we are more pitiable than all men.

20. But now Christos has been raised from [the] dead, [the] firstfruit of the [ones] having fallen asleep.

21. For since through a man [came] death, also through a man [came the] resurrection of [the] dead.

22. For just as in the Adam all die, so also in the Christos all will be made alive.

23. But each in the own order: [the] firstfruit Christos, then the [ones] of the Christos at the coming of him,

24. then the end, when he delivers the kingdom to the God and Father, when he abolishes every rule and every authority and power.

25. For he must reign until he has placed all the enemies under the feet of him.

26. [The] last enemy [to be] abolished [is] the death.

27. For "he subjected all [things] under the feet of him." But when he says that all [things] have been subjected, [it is] clear that [this is] except the [one] having subjected all [things] to him.

28. And when all [things] are subjected to him, then also the Son himself will be subjected to the [one] having subjected all [things] to him, that God may be all in all.

29. Otherwise, what will the [ones] do, the [ones] being baptized for the dead? If [the] dead are not raised at all, why indeed are they baptized for them?

30. Why also do we face danger every hour?

31. I die daily — I swear by the boasting of you, which I have in Christos Yeshua the Lord of us.

32. If according to man I fought with beasts in Ephesos, what [is] the benefit to me? If [the] dead are not raised, "Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die."

33. Do not be deceived: "Bad associations corrupt good morals."

34. Sober up righteously and do not sin; for some have [an] ignorance of God — I say [this] to [the] shame of you.

35. But someone will say, "How are the dead raised? And with what kind of body do they come?"

36. Fool! What you sow is not made alive unless it dies.

37. And what you sow — you do not sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, perhaps of wheat or of some of the rest.

38. But God gives to it a body just as he willed, and to each of the seeds its own body.

39. Not all flesh [is] the same flesh, but one [is that] of men, and another flesh of beasts, and another flesh of birds, and another of fish.

40. And [there are] heavenly bodies and earthly bodies; but different [is] the glory of the heavenly and different [that] of the earthly.

41. One glory of [the] sun, and another glory of [the] moon, and another glory of [the] stars; for star differs from star in glory.

42. Thus also [is] the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruption.

43. It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.

44. It is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual [body].

45. Thus also it has been written, "The first man Adam became a living soul"; the last Adam [became] a life-giving spirit.

46. But not first the spiritual, but the natural; then the spiritual.

47. The first man [is] from [the] earth, earthy; the second man [is] from heaven.

48. As [is] the earthy, such also [are] the earthy [ones]; and as [is] the heavenly, such also [are] the heavenly [ones].

49. And just as we bore the image of the earthy, we will also bear the image of the heavenly.

50. And this I say, brothers, that flesh and blood is not able to inherit [the] kingdom of God, nor does the corruption inherit the incorruption.

51. Behold, I tell you a mystery: we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,

52. in a moment, in [the] blink of an eye, at the last trumpet; for the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.

53. For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

54. And when this corruptible puts on incorruption and this mortal puts on immortality, then will come to pass the word that has been written: "The death was swallowed up in victory."

55. "Where, O death, [is] the victory of you? Where, O death, [is] the sting of you?"

56. And the sting of the death [is] the sin, and the power of the sin [is] the law.

57. But thanks [be] to God, the [one] giving us the victory through the Lord of us Yeshua Christos.

58. So then, beloved brothers of me, become steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that the labor of you is not empty in [the] Lord.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **vv.3-5 — The pre-Pauline creed:** This is the earliest Christian creed in written form, predating Paul's letter. The formula "received...delivered" (παρέλαβον...παρέδωκα) indicates formal tradition. The four-line creed: died / buried / raised / appeared — each with corroboration ("according to the Scriptures," witnesses).

2. **v.5 — "the twelve":** τοῖς δώδεκα — used as a title for the apostolic group even though Judas was gone. This indicates "the Twelve" was an established designation.

3. **v.8 — "untimely born" (ἔκτρωμα):** Literally "miscarriage" or "abortion" — an extremely self-deprecating term. Paul sees his apostolic birth as abnormal, violent, premature.

4. **v.29 — "baptized for the dead":** βαπτιζόμενοι ὑπὲρ τῶν νεκρῶν — one of the most obscure verses in the NT. Over 200 interpretations have been proposed. Paul does not endorse the practice but uses it as an argument: even this strange practice presupposes resurrection.

5. **v.45 — Genesis 2:7 citation:** Paul contrasts the first Adam (ψυχὴν ζῶσαν, "living soul") with the last Adam (πνεῦμα ζῳοποιοῦν, "life-giving spirit"). Christ is not merely alive — he gives life.

6. **v.55 — Hosea 13:14:** Paul's triumphant quotation. Sinaiticus reads "death" (θάνατε) in both lines rather than "Hades" (ᾅδη) in the second — a variant from the LXX tradition.

— EMET, AI Translator
